items.title: Global headwinds put electric bike companies on price hike path
items.summary: Electric two-wheeler companies are feeling the pinch. Rising raw material prices and supply chain issues are impacting profits. Companies like Bajaj Auto, Hero MotoCorp, and Ather Energy are warning of price increases. Demand for AI infrastructure is also affecting the supply of crucial components. Consumers may see higher prices for electric vehicles soon.

items.title: Retail inflation up a tad on rise in food, bullion prices
items.summary: Retail inflation reached a four-month peak of 3.5% in April. This rise was fueled by increasing food and gold/silver prices. Economists anticipate further inflationary pressures ahead. The Reserve Bank of India is expected to maintain its current policy rate. Inflation is nearing the RBI's target, suggesting a pause in rate hikes for now.

items.title: NEET-UG 2026 cancelled amid paper leak allegations, re-exam likely in June
items.summary: The National Testing Authority has taken decisive action by canceling the NEET-UG 2026 examination that took place on May 3, following serious allegations of paper leaks. items.title: CEA Nageswaran says West Asia crisis a balance of payments stress test; to impact CAD, inflation & exchange rate
items.summary: Large companies must pay micro and small enterprises on time. This eases their working capital and reduces funding costs. Managing the current account, financing it, and preventing currency depreciation are key macroeconomic goals for FY27. The West Asia crisis presents a balance of payments stress test.

items.title: West Asia crisis a wake-up call for self-reliance: Commerce and industry minister Piyush Goyal
items.summary: The West Asia crisis serves as a crucial reminder for Indian industries to boost self-reliance and efficiency. He also highlighted the need for faster electric vehicle adoption to cut oil bills.

items.title: Box office boom pushes film industry back to theatre-first strategy
items.summary: Indian cinema is returning to a theatrical-first release model. Streaming platforms are reducing spending, and studios are focusing on box office success. Films now arrive on OTT services about eight weeks after their cinema debut. This shift marks a departure from direct-to-digital premieres. Box office collections are rising, while digital licensing demand has weakened.

Recruiters can engage with potential candidates right on campus, making the hiring process streamlined and efficient.

items.title: Kharif reset: Farmers may ditch paddy and maize for soybean, pulses amid monsoon fears
items.summary: As the kharif season approaches, farmers are leaning towards cultivating oilseeds like soybean and pulses such as urad and masur, primarily due to worries about erratic monsoon patterns and looming fertilizer shortages. Following a recent surge, maize planting is forecasted to decline, as farmers shift towards crops that are less fertilizer-dependent and lower in risk amid unpredictable weather conditions.

The minister emphasizes reducing import dependence to bolster the economy. He also highlights the potential of electric vehicles and advanced technologies like AI for growth. items.title: Jhunjhunwala-partnered fund buys stake in TFCI: What's buzzing?
items.summary: In an interview with CNBC-TV18, Satpal Arora, MD of Tourism Finance Corporation of India (TFCI), talked about the state of business and the outlook ahead.

items.title: RBI latest meeting 'diluted' its old policy framework: Nomura
items.summary: On Tuesday, the central bank surprised the market with a 25-basis-point cut to its policy repo rate, or the rate at which it lends to banks, to 6.25 percent.

items.title: Bond bull market may pause but is far from over: Expert
items.summary: The benchmark 10-year government-security yield remained stuck in 8-7.5 percent range through all of 2015 and half of 2016, moving lower to sub-7 percent only when the RBI promised in April to reduce the system's liquidity deficit. The yield may now fall more.

items.title: Scope for meaningful rate cuts going ahead: Neelkanth Mishra
items.summary: Credit Suisse' Neelkanth Mishra expects repo rate to fall to a decade low in the coming quarters. He says that beginning December the market may see a robust and widespread pick-up which may boost indices.

items.title: Tech up to a healthier world
items.summary: Sensors, 3D printed skin, wearables, reconstructed organs, revolutionized clinical trials, personalized medicine and what not – get ready for a new era of healthcare

items.title: Steel stocks rally as govt extends MIP on 66 steel products
items.summary: Stocks like Hindustan Zinc, Hindalco, Jindal Steel, JSW Steel and Tata Steel gained over 1 percent from previous close.

items.title: Endurance Technologies IPO opens: Should you subscribe?
items.summary: The Aurangabad-based company aims to mop up nearly Rs 1,162 crore (at higher end of price band) by diluting 17.5 percent stake through the issue that will close on October 7. It already raised Rs 348.52 crore through anchor investors' portion on Tuesday, the day before issue opening.

items.title: Cautiously optimistic on India; eyeing earnings revival: Sanger
items.summary: Global liquidity tailwinds are unlikely to get any better from now and that makes Arvind Sanger, Managing Partner at Geosphere Capital Management “cautiously optimistic” on the Indian markets.

items.title: Automation threatens 69% jobs in India: World Bank
items.summary: "In large parts of Africa, it is likely that technology could fundamentally disrupt this pattern. Research based on World Bank data has predicted that the proportion of jobs threatened in India by automation is 69 percent, in China it is 77 percent and in Ethiopia, the percentage of jobs threatened by automation is 85 percent," he said.
